reasoning: France is the clear and lengthening outright favorite after sweeping Group I 10-2, with the market pricing them around +360 and roughly a 20% title chance; their attacking depth (Mbappé plus Ballon d'Or winner Dembélé, who scored a 32-minute hat trick against Norway) and an elite Maignan-Saliba-Upamecano spine make them the strongest squad on paper. Argentina, the defending champions led by an in-form Messi, have surged to second favorite on the back of confident wins and a favorable Round-of-32 draw against Cape Verde, making a 2022 final rematch a live possibility. Spain (Euro 2024 champions) and England round out the top tier, with Brazil under Ancelotti finding form after a slow start but still carrying defensive question marks. Morocco is my dark horse: they topped a group containing Brazil-level competition on 7 points and reached the 2022 semifinals, giving them the pedigree to upset a heavyweight.
